title: Structured Retrieval, Knowledge Graphs, GraphRAG, and Multi-Hop Evidence · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved trace contains a plausible provenance-preserving multi-hop retriever with one ingestion, normalization, chunking, lexical, embedding, index, filter, fusion, reranking, context, generation, citation, authorization, latency, or recovery defect; identify the first failed stage before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in provenance-preserving multi-hop retriever and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Choose relational, document, graph, lexical, vector, or combined retrieval from question structure, source authority, joins, constraints, and evidence needs.
Use validated schemas, parameterized queries, authorization, row provenance, aggregation grain, and textual evidence when questions require structured facts.
Represent entities, relations, qualifiers, time, source spans, confidence, conflicts, schema, and extraction version and distinguish asserted from generated edges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug RAG engineering team handles case 1981 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to select structured, SQL, knowledge-graph, or GraphRAG retrieval only when multi-hop evidence beats baseline; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: rag-multihop-path-evidence-1xlg52
# Competency: rag-multihop-path-evidence.
# Case 5806: a public-benefits policy change.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall.
def evidence_rag_multihop_path_evidence_1xlg52(entities: int = 12, relations: int = 18, hops: int = 2, provenance_edges: int = 18, schema_valid: bool = True, baseline_beaten: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1xlg52 = "1xlg52-25glx1-5806"
    assert evidence_variant_1xlg52
    evidence_axes_1xlg52 = (
        "corpus1xlg52",
        "query25glx1",
        "tenant5806",
        "index1xlg525806",
        "prompt25glx15806",
        "evaluation58061xlg52",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1xlg52)) == 6
    if entities <= 0 or relations < 0:
        return False, 0, "graph-shape"
    if hops < 1 or hops > 4:
        return False, hops, "hop-budget"
    if provenance_edges < relations:
        return False, provenance_edges, "edge-provenance"
    if not schema_valid:
        return False, relations, "structured-contract"
    if not baseline_beaten:
        return False, relations, "complexity-not-earned"
    assert provenance_edges >= relations
    return True, hops, "grounded-multihop"
